Site.name: Good Samaritan International
Site.motto: Let all that you do be done in love

Footer.summary: A small board, faithful donors, and families we know by name.

hero.headline: Walking with Romanian families since 1994.
hero.subhead: In 2024 we sent nearly $100,000 to named families for groceries, medicine, firewood, and home repair. Ninety-eight cents of every dollar reached the field.

trustBand.label: Three things to know about us
trustBand.stat1Detail: Fifty-plus named families. Every dollar tagged by category.
trustBand.stat2Detail: No paid staff, ever. Board members pay their own travel.
trustBand.stat3Label: Of every dollar to the field
trustBand.stat3Detail: All giving goes to families, missionaries, and projects.
trustBand.stat4Detail: IRS EIN 38-3401779. Tax-deductible. Annual report public.

projects.heading: Where 2024 went, in five categories
projects.housingBody: The Dragomir family received the keys to their home in September 2023. In 2024 we helped the Anton family insulate theirs before winter, and connected the Barbalata home to electrical service.
projects.medicalBody: Marta Lutac is undergoing cancer treatment in Rauseni. Her grandson Bogdan had two eye surgeries this year. We covered medicine and hospital travel for both.
projects.firewoodBody: The Ciobanasu family in Avrameni and the Murariu family in Moldova received firewood and supplies before the cold.

story.heading: Real families. By name.
story.title: The Lutac family, Rauseni
story.excerpt: Marta is in treatment. Her grandson Bogdan needed two eye surgeries this year. The board prayed with the family this summer and covered medicine and hospital travel.

newsletter.heading: A letter from Romania, four times a year
newsletter.body: Updates from the board's visits and the families we walk with. We email when there's something real to say.
newsletter.trust: No tracking pixels. Unsubscribe in one click.

intro.headline: Where every dollar lands.
intro.subhead: Choose a category below, or let us put your gift where the need is greatest this week. Ninety-eight cents of every dollar reaches the field; the board pays its own travel.

funds.generalBody: Let the board direct your gift to the family most in need this week.
funds.housingBody: Home repair, insulation, electrical service. The Dragomir, Anton, and Barbalata families this year.
funds.medicalBody: Surgeries, medicine, hospital travel. Marta Lutac's treatment and Bogdan's eye surgeries this year.
funds.firewoodBody: Firewood, utility bills, winter supplies. The Ciobanasu and Murariu families before the cold.
funds.missionBody: Missionary support in Romania, the UK, the US, and Africa.
funds.schoolBody: School supplies and tuition aid in Detroit, Rwanda, and Romania.

tiers.tier25: A week of groceries for one family.
tiers.tier50: A month of firewood when the temperature drops.
tiers.tier100: Medicine and supplies for one hospital visit.
tiers.tier250: Insulating one wall before winter, like the Anton family.
tiers.tier500: Connecting a home to electrical service, like the Barbalata family.

cadence.monthlyHelp: Recurring on the same date each month. Cancel anytime in one click.
cadence.onceHelp: Single gift, with a receipt in your inbox in under a minute.

otherWays.heading: Prefer to send it directly?
otherWays.zelleHeading: Zelle (we prefer this, no card fees)
otherWays.zelleBody: Send to gsworldoutreach@gmail.com from your bank app. The board sees your gift the same day.
otherWays.checkBody: Make payable to: Good Samaritan. Mail to 2060 Curtis Rd, Addison Twp, MI 48307. Allow seven to ten days.

trust.cancelAnytime: Cancel monthly giving anytime in one click.
trust.secureProcessing: Card processing by Stripe via Givebutter.

Donate.receiptBody: Thank you. Your gift reaches a named Romanian family this week. We are grateful for you.

hero.headline: Romania mission trips.
hero.subhead: We do not have a 2026 trip scheduled yet. If you would consider going with us when one is set, add your name to the interest list below.

history.heading: What past trips have looked like
history.body: Our most recent board visit was summer 2024. The board flew to Bucharest, drove through Botosani and Brasov counties, and sat with the Lutac, Anton, Barbalata, Ciobanasu, Maicaru, Ghete, Curavale, Murariu, and Petru Vlascu families across about ten days. Photos and a per-family log are in the 2024 annual report.

skills.heading: Skills that help on the ground

cost.heading: Expected cost
cost.body: Volunteers cover their own flight (typically $900 to $1,400 from the US) and contribute $500 to $800 for ground costs (lodging, food, transport). Total falls in the $1,500 to $2,500 range. Board members pay their own travel separately.

apply.heading: Join the interest list
apply.body: Email gsworldoutreach@gmail.com with the subject "2026 trip interest" and a sentence about why. The board writes back when dates lock, expected by summer 2026.

hero.headline: Hands we could use stateside.
hero.subhead: We are a small all-volunteer board. A few specific roles would multiply what we can do without flying anywhere.

contact.heading: Reach the board
contact.body: Email gsworldoutreach@gmail.com with the role that fits you, or use the contact form.

hero.headline: Reach the board directly.
hero.subhead: Every message goes to the same email the board reads daily. Someone responds within seven days.

form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us as much or as little as you would like.
form.successHeading: Thank you. The board has the message.
form.successBody: Someone from the board responds within seven days. If it is urgent, write to gsworldoutreach@gmail.com directly.

hero.headline: Founded in 1994. Still walking with the same families.
hero.subhead: Good Samaritan International began the year Nistor and Floare Forgaciu started taking groceries and firewood to widows and elderly neighbors in their corner of Romania. Thirty years later, the relationships are still by name.

team.heading: Our team
team.lead: Every member of our team is a volunteer. No one on this board is compensated.
team.body: We take our convictions and turn them into action. Every board member volunteers their time and effort for planning and implementing projects. Travel to Romania is paid out of our own pockets. None of your gift ever pays a salary, and nothing ever has. That has been true since 1994 and we have not seen a reason to change it.
team.stat3Label: Of every dollar to the field

history.heading: Our history
history.body: Nistor and Floare Forgaciu founded the organization in 1994 with one conviction: that following Jesus meant remembering the poor. They started small, in their own region, carrying groceries and firewood to widows and elderly neighbors. The work has grown across three decades but the way it works has not changed. The board still visits each named family in person, prays with them in their kitchens, and stays in touch through the local pastors who first introduced them.

mission.heading: Our mission
mission.body: To help and serve widows, orphans, families, and local church communities by the grace of God with a humble and obedient heart.

faith.heading: What we believe
faith.body: We are a Christian 501(c)(3). Our work is rooted in the call of the apostles to remember the poor (Galatians 2:10). We partner with local churches in every region we serve. The families we walk with are not projects to be completed but neighbors to be loved.

where.heading: Where we serve
where.body: Romania carries 76% of 2024 aid, with the United States at 16%, the United Kingdom at 5%, and Africa at 3%. Past years have reached Moldova, Canada, Spain, Uganda, Haiti, Rwanda, and Greece.

accountability.heading: Accountability
accountability.body: Every board member is a volunteer. Every trip to Romania is paid out of pocket. The 2024 annual report names families, villages, and project categories so every dollar is traceable.
accountability.irsLink: Verify our 501(c)(3) status with the IRS

contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.body: Email gsworldoutreach@gmail.com or write to 2060 Curtis Rd, Addison Twp, MI 48307.
